ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: I ti ka nyɛm mbaa bo bu dɔ i wɔ. Yiri ti alɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ kaan durunya n nu a.
translated: You and your brother will not get the thing. The house of your family is far from the river and the village.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Ama, mini, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má am tan ndiɛ mmɔɔ fieo. Má am tan aŋgoro, dama Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n woo­biri lɛɛ.
translated: They said, "Well, we will go to the river, and then we will go home." We went to the river, but God sent a heavy rain.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Na ba ŋmati abara ayɛ miɛm n kɛrɛ su, na deke kɛrɛ bo bu tiiri i ŋga, bu sɔ bu tukpaki­fɔm ni bu dasum kɛrɛ ba i dɔ ɔ.
translated: They took the calabash and put water in it, and then they put the yam in and covered it with a leaf and they put the stick on top of it and they carried it on their head.

ref: The food that you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: Bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i so i sui na i ti.]”
translated: When you are in the village and you see a stranger coming towards you, you should know that you are the one he is coming to meet and you should be prepared.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Saŋga bo wɔmbu n nya di má durunya n ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ woori di ni nu su wɔ, na nbaram ni nbiɛsɔm suja bu beŋgum, fa kɔ juuri haari cɛɛn n bo Nowa wuraari lee bambaka n nu ni.
translated: The chief of the village and his people were in the house, the guests were outside, and the children were playing, when a big storm came, and the wind blew the roof of the house of the chief of Nowa and destroyed it completely.

ref: Pilate set Barabbas free. Then he ordered his soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip and nail him to a cross. Soldiers Make Fun of Jesus (Mark
text: Dɔ na Gomina Pilato ayi Barabasi aji, na wɔ ma sojam n afin Yesu ŋgbere, na wɔ nya afa i awura bu saa nu ka bu kɔ kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu. Kabo sojam yoori Yesu anzasi ni
translated: Then Governor Pilate took Barabbas and handed Jesus over to be flogged, and they wove a crown of thorns and put it on his head and put a purple robe on him, and they mocked him and spat on him and beat him on the head with a reed and knelt down in homage to him. And the soldiers led Jesus away inside

ref: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
text: Na wɔ se i nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm bo bu wo i nyunu n ka, “Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n lɛɛ, ye wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu a, nyini ti ye i la yiko koro yo nyini alekutura sam kɛrɛ a.”
translated: They said to the lame man, "John the Baptist sent us to ask you, 'Are you the one who is to come, or should we expect someone else?'"

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Na ba ba Yesu dɔ, na ba ŋu sɔnɔ n bo ajinim n yo woori i ŋu n ka i tana Yesu bi wura tanlɛɛ, ni i nyunu koroya su, na sɛrɛ ati bu.
translated: When they saw Jesus, they ran to tell the news to the people in the town that Jesus, the son of God, had come, and that he was going to the river, and they all followed him.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: na ba wo dɔ haari Fɛmɛ Herode aba awu. Ahin yoori sɔ ni, i ma kabo Micɛra n ma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n yo jɔjɔɔri n yo yii­wa a. I ŋa, “Ma fere m wa n afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su.” Kabo Fɛmɛ Herode ma bu kuunri nbaa­biɛsɔ kan kanm ni
translated: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod, Magi from the east came to Jerusalem and asked, "Where is the one who has been born king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him." When King Herod heard this he was disturbed, and all Jerusalem with him.

ref: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
text: Dɔ na bɛrɛ bo bu wo m saa biɛ su ni nyá bɔ m nu ka, ‘Ya Mibiɛ, saŋga bɔnɔ ye ya ŋuuri u ka ahɔɛ ati u, wara nzuehɔɛ ati u, wara a baari ya wɔfɔ, wara a kaari u bu ŋgbɛɛn, wara a daari tukpaki, wara a woori dan­saraka, ye ya buka má u a?’
translated: They went and met a certain woman on the road and said to her, 'Grandmother, what is the thing that you are going to do to us, will you burn us, will you peel us, will you pound us in a mortar, will you grind us on a grinding stone, will you roast us on a fire, will you throw us into a hole, what are you going to do to us?

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say that Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Na ba bisa Yesu ka, “Ye nzu ti ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n bita jɔjɔ ka Eliya dú mɔ ba ka, ye Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni bá ba n a?”
translated: They asked Jesus, "Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Na Petoro afa i akɔ ahɛmɛ, na wɔ jaraki i ase i ka, “M Mibiɛ, má a se ka a se sɔ, Nyɛmɛ yo u kisi ni nyini sa ni. Nyini koro yo má cɛɛn be da.”
translated: Petoro went to the village, and they welcomed him, saying, "Mibiɛ, don't say what you want to say, God is with you and your wife too. Your wife will give birth to a child."

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Fɛmɛ n tiiri sɔ ni, na i ahore asaaki agaya, ama, ndiɛ n bo i taanri i jama n nyunu n dɛɛ ti, wɔ koro akaaki má i nɔɔ.
translated: When the war is over and you return home, if you don't bring something, even though you have money in your pocket, they will say you are poor.

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Saŋga bo Yohane susu­fɔm kaaki sukɔ ni, na Yesu ajɔjɔ jama n dɔ, fa kɔ Yohane ŋusu jɔrɛ, i ŋa, “Saŋga bo am fite kɔɔri Yohane dɔ, boro kpantain n nu ni, am kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu nzu wɔ? Am kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu sɔnɔ bo i ti ka barankpaati fofoe bo aŋuma n kankan i wɔ?
translated: The crowd that was with John was watching, and they asked Jesus, "John's disciples often fast and pray, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours go on eating and drinking. You say John's disciples fast often and yours go on eating and drinking, why do your disciples not fast like the Pharisees and others do?

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Bɛrɛ bo bu ahore­mbaa ti casi ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama bɛrɛ bá ŋu Nyɛmɛ a.
translated: The people of the village gathered at the crossroads, they sat under the tree, and the people asked Nyɛmɛ a question.

ref: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
text: Má kampenta n wa n lɛɛ? Má i ni la Mariya a? Má Yakobo, ni Yosefu, ni Simon, ni Yudasi n gɔrɔ n lɛɛ?
translated: Isn't this the carpenter's son? Isn't his mother's name Mary, and aren't his brothers James, Joseph, Simon and Judas?

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Na ba ba Yesu dɔ, na ba ŋu sɔnɔ n bo ajinim n yo woori i ŋu n ka i tana Yesu bi wura tanlɛɛ, ni i nyunu koroya su, na sɛrɛ ati bu.
translated: When they saw Jesus, they ran to tell the news to the people in the town that Jesus, the son of God, had come, and that he was going to the river, and they all followed him.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Nzɛn i mibiɛ n ba ŋuuri ka i suyo i juma kabo i seeri ka i yo ni, tere­fiɛ wo nyini akɔɔ n dɔ.
translated: When I was a child my father took me and showed me the forest and told me that, this forest is full of evil spirits.

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Saŋga bo gbɛfɛnɛ n tiiri nyini sɔ ni, na wɔ yasu akɔ ni ahoresaaki, dama i la neŋge agaya n dɛɛ ti.
translated: The woman and the man are in the house, and they are sharing the property, because it is for all of them.

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Am acɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n ase buekun, na am nya susu adamande dɛɛ sa­yoo­wam su.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, "I will make you fishers of men, and you will catch many people for salvation."
